---
title: "Tech Trends (EN) - 2026-02-28"
author: uma-chan
categories:
  - "auto"
  - "tech-trends"
  - "en"
date: 2026-02-28
date-modified: last-modified
description: |
  Daily tech trend digest (EN) - Hacker News, GitHub Trending, Reddit
image: "/assets/common/icon_hhkb3_large.jpg"
---

## Picks For You

- [ ] **[OpenAI raises $110B on $730B pre-money valuation](https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/27/openai-raises-110b-in-one-of-the-largest-private-funding-rounds-in-history/)** - OpenAI closed what is reportedly one of the largest private funding rounds in history, raising $110B at a $730B pre-money valuation. The scale underscores just how much capital is flowing into frontier AI development. [ai]
- [ ] **[kibitz – Real-time decoded feed of AI agent actions](https://github.com/kibitzsh/kibitz)** - Monitor multiple Claude Code and Codex sessions from a single dashboard, seeing exactly what each agent is doing and coordinating swarms efficiently. 195 GitHub stars on day one. [claude-code, developer-tools]
- [ ] **[agentchattr – Local chat server for AI coding agent coordination](https://github.com/bcurts/agentchattr)** - A local real-time chat server enabling autonomous coordination between multiple AI coding agents and humans, making multi-agent workflows more observable. [claude-code, ai]
- [ ] **[6551Team/opennews-mcp – Crypto News MCP server](https://github.com/6551Team/opennews-mcp)** - MCP server aggregating crypto news with AI-powered ratings and trading signals, providing real-time updates for LLM-integrated trading workflows. 509 GitHub stars. [mcp]
- [ ] **[6551Team/opentwitter-mcp – Twitter/X Data MCP server](https://github.com/6551Team/opentwitter-mcp)** - MCP server exposing Twitter/X data, user profiles, tweet search, follower events, and KOL tracking directly to LLM agents. 278 GitHub stars. [mcp]
- [ ] **[Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL – Personalize AI by talking to it](https://github.com/Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL)** - Reinforcement learning framework that lets users personalize an AI agent simply by conversing with it, earning 318 GitHub stars on debut. [ai]
- [ ] **[GitHub Copilot CLI downloads and executes malware](https://www.promptarmor.com/resources/github-copilot-cli-downloads-and-executes-malware)** - PromptArmor published a vulnerability disclosure showing GitHub Copilot CLI can be prompt-injected into downloading and executing malicious payloads. Urgent reading for teams using Copilot in CI/CD pipelines. [developer-tools, security]

## AI & Machine Learning

- [ ] **[Smallest transformer that can add two 10-digit numbers](https://github.com/anadim/AdderBoard)** - A research experiment determining the minimal transformer architecture capable of reliably adding two 10-digit integers, offering insight into what arithmetic truly requires from attention mechanisms. [ai, machine-learning]
- [ ] **[A Chinese official's use of ChatGPT revealed an intimidation operation](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/25/politics/chatgpt-china-intimidation-operation)** - CNN reports that a Chinese government official's ChatGPT prompts inadvertently exposed the mechanics of a state-sponsored intimidation campaign, a landmark case for AI-assisted intelligence analysis. [ai, security]
- [ ] **[The Token Economy](https://dev.to/dannwaneri/the-token-economy-3cd9)** - An examination of how LLM token economics are reshaping software business models, pricing strategies, and the hidden costs of AI-native applications. [ai]
- [ ] **[xstongxue/best-prompts – High-quality prompt collection](https://github.com/xstongxue/best-prompts)** - A curated collection of high-quality general-purpose prompts for LLMs, trending with 383 GitHub stars. Practical resource for teams standardizing their AI interaction patterns. [ai, developer-tools]
- [ ] **[My Take On AI and Technology (Beyond the Pure Developer PoV)](https://dev.to/luftietheanonymous/my-take-on-ai-and-technology-besides-the-pure-developer-pov-cce)** - A broader perspective on AI's societal implications that goes beyond code generation, examining how non-engineers experience the current transformation. [ai, essay]

## Development & Tools

- [ ] **[16 Modern JavaScript Features That Might Blow Your Mind](https://dev.to/sylwia-lask/16-modern-javascript-features-that-might-blow-your-mind-4h5e)** - A roundup of 16 JavaScript features many developers overlook, covering recent ECMAScript additions that significantly reduce boilerplate in modern applications. [javascript, web-development]
- [ ] **[The $0 Developer Phase — And How Dev.to Pulled Me Out](https://dev.to/art_light/the-0-developer-phase-and-how-devto-pulled-me-out-84g)** - A candid reflection on hitting a career and motivation floor as a developer, and the community dynamics that helped pull out of it. Topped dev.to reactions this week. [developer-tools, essay]
- [ ] **[I ported Manim to TypeScript (run 3Blue1Brown math animations in the browser)](https://github.com/maloyan/manim-web)** - Manim, the Python library behind 3Blue1Brown's math animations, ported to TypeScript so animations can run directly in the browser without a backend. [typescript, developer-tools]
- [ ] **[Lakr233/vphone-cli – Virtual phone CLI tool](https://github.com/Lakr233/vphone-cli)** - A CLI tool for managing virtual phone operations, trending with 293 GitHub stars. [developer-tools]
- [ ] **[ringhyacinth/Star-Office-UI](https://github.com/ringhyacinth/Star-Office-UI)** - A Star Wars-themed office UI component library, trending with 234 GitHub stars. [web-development]
- [ ] **[olucasandrade/youjustneedpostgres – PostgreSQL is enough](https://github.com/olucasandrade/youjustneedpostgres)** - A reference project arguing that PostgreSQL covers the majority of database use cases, pushing back against premature adoption of specialized databases. 138 GitHub stars. [databases, developer-tools]
- [ ] **[Stop Ignoring RFC 2324 — The Most Important Protocol You've Never Implemented](https://dev.to/pascal_cescato_692b7a8a20/stop-ignoring-rfc-2324-its-the-most-important-protocol-youve-never-implemented-53pe)** - A playful deep-dive into the Hyper Text Coffee Pot Control Protocol (HTCPCP), using it as a lens to discuss how protocol design principles translate to real API design. [web-development]
- [ ] **[The Developer I'm Grateful I Never Became](https://dev.to/narnaiezzsshaa/the-developer-im-grateful-i-never-became-255d)** - A reflection on career inflection points and the developer anti-patterns that are easy to drift into — cynicism, stagnation, over-specialization — and how to avoid them. [developer-tools, essay]

## Data Platform

- [ ] **[dbt-labs/dbt-mcp – dbt MCP server](https://github.com/dbt-labs/dbt-mcp)** - Official dbt MCP server enabling LLM agents to interact with dbt projects, run models, and query results directly through the Model Context Protocol. [dbt, mcp]
- [ ] **[Agent Skills for Data Engineering (Airflow, dbt, Analytics)](https://github.com/astronomer/agents)** - Astronomer released agent skills for data engineering workflows covering Airflow, dbt, and analytics operations, enabling AI-assisted pipeline management. [dbt, ai]
- [ ] **[astronomer/astronomer-cosmos – Run dbt in Airflow](https://github.com/astronomer/astronomer-cosmos)** - Cosmos allows running dbt Core projects as Airflow DAGs with native dependency resolution and observability, bridging the orchestration and transformation layers. [dbt, databricks]
- [ ] **[AltimateAI/vscode-dbt-power-user – dbt Power User VS Code extension](https://github.com/AltimateAI/vscode-dbt-power-user)** - Feature-rich VS Code extension for dbt development with AI-assisted query generation, lineage visualization, and test management. [dbt, developer-tools]
- [ ] **[elementary-data/elementary – dbt data observability](https://github.com/elementary-data/elementary)** - Open-source dbt-native data observability platform with anomaly detection, freshness monitoring, and Slack alerting built on top of dbt tests. [dbt]
- [ ] **[elementary-data/dbt-data-reliability](https://github.com/elementary-data/dbt-data-reliability)** - Data reliability package from the Elementary team that extends dbt testing with probabilistic anomaly detection and automated threshold learning. [dbt]
- [ ] **[DataRecce/recce – dbt PR review tool](https://github.com/DataRecce/recce)** - Recce automates dbt pull request reviews by diffing query results between environments, making data change validation part of the standard PR workflow. [dbt]
- [ ] **[lightdash/lightdash – BI tool built on dbt](https://github.com/lightdash/lightdash)** - Open-source BI tool that reads your dbt project definitions directly, turning dbt models and metrics into dashboards without re-defining business logic. [dbt]
- [ ] **[sodadata/soda-core – Data quality testing](https://github.com/sodadata/soda-core)** - Framework-agnostic data quality testing library that integrates with dbt, Spark, and most SQL warehouses for SLA monitoring and anomaly detection. [dbt]
- [ ] **[godatadriven/dbt-bouncer – dbt governance linting](https://github.com/godatadriven/dbt-bouncer)** - Configurable linting tool that enforces governance rules on dbt projects: naming conventions, documentation coverage, test coverage, and more. [dbt]
- [ ] **[DataTalksClub/data-engineering-zoomcamp](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/data-engineering-zoomcamp)** - The free data engineering course covering dbt, BigQuery, Spark, and Kafka with hands-on projects — consistently one of the best entry points into the modern data stack. [dbt, bigquery, databricks]
- [ ] **[tconbeer/sqlfmt – SQL formatter for dbt](https://github.com/tconbeer/sqlfmt)** - Opinionated SQL formatter designed specifically for dbt projects, with Jinja-aware formatting and CI integration. [dbt]
- [ ] **[databricks/dbt-databricks – dbt adapter for Databricks](https://github.com/databricks/dbt-databricks)** - Official dbt adapter for Databricks, supporting Unity Catalog, Delta Live Tables integration, and Databricks-specific SQL optimizations. [dbt, databricks]
- [ ] **[wvlet/wvlet – SQL-first data transformation](https://github.com/wvlet/wvlet)** - A SQL-first data transformation language and framework designed to make complex data pipelines more readable and maintainable. [dbt]
- [ ] **[Qualifier Placeholders: Decoupling SQL from Schema Names](https://medium.com/@jacovanderlaan/qualifier-placeholders-decoupling-sql-from-schema-names-fcf3d6a01184?source=rss------dbt-5)** - A technique for making dbt SQL portable across environments by abstracting schema references into configurable qualifiers instead of hardcoding them. [dbt]
- [ ] **[SQL-First Modeling: Write SQL, Get Metadata for Free](https://medium.com/towards-data-engineering/sql-first-modeling-write-sql-get-metadata-for-free-92d2e6a3fda8?source=rss------dbt-5)** - An approach to data modeling where SQL definitions automatically generate metadata, lineage, and documentation without separate annotation steps. [dbt]
- [ ] **[AutomateDV Deep Dive: Metadata-Driven Data Vault 2.0 at Scale with dbt](https://medium.com/@sendoamoronta/automatedv-deep-dive-metadata-driven-data-vault-2-0-at-scale-with-dbt-9b96b8d885a2?source=rss------dbt-5)** - A detailed walkthrough of implementing Data Vault 2.0 patterns at scale using the AutomateDV dbt package, covering hub, link, and satellite loading strategies. [dbt]
- [ ] **[Run dbt Models on a Fabric Warehouse](https://medium.com/@sindu0090/run-dbt-models-on-a-fabric-warehouse-679f4d9da68d?source=rss------dbt-5)** - Step-by-step guide to configuring dbt to run against Microsoft Fabric's Synapse Analytics warehouse, expanding dbt's reach into the Microsoft data ecosystem. [dbt]
- [ ] **[Running single dbt operations in Orchestra](https://medium.com/orchestras-data-release-pipeline-blog/running-single-dbt-operations-in-orchestra-c780a94c025d?source=rss------dbt-5)** - How to use Orchestra's pipeline orchestration to trigger individual dbt model runs on-demand, useful for event-driven pipeline architectures. [dbt]
- [ ] **[AI-Assisted Testing: Why Requirements Matter More, Not Less](https://medium.com/@jacovanderlaan/ai-assisted-testing-why-requirements-matter-more-not-less-d79935e7540d?source=rss------dbt-5)** - Argues that AI-generated test cases make clear requirements more critical, not less — because AI amplifies the quality of what you specify. [dbt, ai]
- [ ] **[Data Vault Migration Strategy — From Ensemble Modeling to Canonical Structures](https://medium.com/@jacovanderlaan/data-vault-migration-strategy-from-ensemble-modeling-to-canonical-structures-bab2d331b03b?source=rss------dbt-5)** - A migration guide for evolving Data Vault implementations from ensemble modeling patterns toward canonical hub-and-satellite structures. [dbt]
- [ ] **[From Raw CSVs to a Dimensional Model with dbt on MovieLens 20M](https://medium.com/@adams-chang/from-raw-csvs-to-a-dimensional-model-what-building-a-dbt-pipeline-on-movielens-20m-actually-taught-5eac86a57bd9?source=rss------dbt-5)** - A practical tutorial building a full dbt-based dimensional model on the MovieLens 20M dataset, with lessons learned about incremental models and testing strategies. [dbt]
- [ ] **[airbytehq/airbyte – Open-source data integration](https://github.com/airbytehq/airbyte)** - The leading open-source ELT platform with 300+ connectors, providing the ingestion layer that feeds most modern Snowflake and cloud warehouse setups. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[tobymao/sqlglot – SQL transpiler and formatter](https://github.com/tobymao/sqlglot)** - Python SQL transpiler that converts SQL between dialects including Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, Spark, and more — critical for cross-platform portability. [snowflake, bigquery, databricks]
- [ ] **[snowflakedb/snowflake-cli – Snowflake CLI](https://github.com/snowflakedb/snowflake-cli)** - Official CLI for Snowflake, enabling deployment of Snowpark applications, Streamlit apps, and Snowflake Native Apps from the terminal. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[snowflakedb/snowpark-python – Snowpark Python](https://github.com/snowflakedb/snowpark-python)** - Snowflake's Python library for building data pipelines and ML workflows that run directly in Snowflake compute, eliminating data movement. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[open-metadata/OpenMetadata – Unified data discovery and governance](https://github.com/open-metadata/OpenMetadata)** - Comprehensive open-source data catalog supporting discovery, lineage, quality, and governance across Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, and other platforms. [snowflake, bigquery, databricks]
- [ ] **[Playing with Snowflake-managed MCP](https://medium.com/snowflake/playing-with-snowflake-managed-mcp-0d05a3962ee9?source=rss------snowflake-5)** - A hands-on exploration of Snowflake's managed MCP server, which exposes Snowflake data and compute capabilities to LLM agents through the Model Context Protocol. [snowflake, mcp]
- [ ] **[Run Claude Code with Snowflake LLM Models via REST API](https://haebichan.medium.com/run-claude-code-with-snowflake-llm-models-via-rest-api-789ca19de2cf?source=rss------snowflake-5)** - Tutorial showing how to configure Claude Code to use Snowflake-hosted LLM models via REST API, enabling Claude Code workflows within Snowflake's compute environment. [snowflake, claude-code]
- [ ] **[Snowflake RBAC: Stop Granting Permissions to People](https://dataengineeringinprogress.medium.com/snowflake-rbac-stop-granting-permissions-to-people-72b7b3d116ac?source=rss------snowflake-5)** - A guide to proper Snowflake RBAC design using role-based inheritance instead of user-level grants, reducing permission sprawl and audit complexity. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[Building Intelligent Data Models with Snowflake Semantic View Autopilot](https://snowflakechronicles.medium.com/building-intelligent-data-models-with-snowflake-semantic-view-autopilot-12dcdd999765?source=rss------snowflake-5)** - Snowflake Semantic View Autopilot automatically generates semantic layer definitions from existing SQL, reducing manual effort in maintaining business-logic metadata. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[How Medidata Cut Compute Costs by 80% Using Snowflake and Apache Iceberg](https://medium.com/snowflake/how-medidata-cut-compute-costs-by-80-using-snowflake-and-apache-iceberg-2194aaee7d54?source=rss------snowflake-5)** - Case study on Medidata's migration from proprietary Snowflake storage to open Apache Iceberg tables, achieving 80% compute cost reduction while retaining Snowflake query performance. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[Snowflake's New Feature: AI-Powered Data Quality Checks in Snowsight](https://dev.to/sagara/snowflakes-new-feature-ai-powered-data-quality-checks-you-can-set-up-directly-in-snowsight-2mpm)** - Snowflake added AI-assisted data quality rule suggestions directly in the Snowsight UI, lowering the barrier to setting up automated quality monitoring. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[Setting Up a Robust Local DevX for Snowflake Python Development](https://dev.to/superpayments/setting-up-a-robust-local-devx-for-snowflake-python-development-12pb)** - A team's approach to local Snowflake development with hot-reload, test isolation, and CI parity using Snowpark and containerized environments. [snowflake, python]
- [ ] **[ibis-project/ibis – Python data analysis framework](https://github.com/ibis-project/ibis)** - Backend-agnostic Python dataframe library that compiles to SQL for BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks, and 15+ other engines — the pandas-compatible path to warehouse-native compute. [snowflake, bigquery, databricks]
- [ ] **[bruin-data/ingestr – Data ingestion CLI](https://github.com/bruin-data/ingestr)** - Single CLI for ingesting data from any source to any destination including Snowflake and BigQuery, without writing pipeline code. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[PeerDB-io/peerdb – Postgres to Snowflake/BigQuery replication](https://github.com/PeerDB-io/peerdb)** - High-performance CDC replication from PostgreSQL to cloud warehouses including Snowflake and BigQuery, with sub-minute latency and automatic schema evolution. [snowflake, bigquery]
- [ ] **[cube-js/cube – Semantic layer for cloud warehouses](https://github.com/cube-js/cube)** - Headless BI semantic layer that works with Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks to expose consistent metrics and dimensions to any downstream tool. [snowflake]
- [ ] **[delta-io/delta-rs – Delta Lake for Python and Rust](https://github.com/delta-io/delta-rs)** - Native Rust/Python implementation of the Delta Lake protocol, enabling Delta table reads/writes without a JVM, critical for lightweight Databricks ecosystem tooling. [databricks]
- [ ] **[databricks/terraform-provider-databricks](https://github.com/databricks/terraform-provider-databricks)** - Official Terraform provider for Databricks, enabling infrastructure-as-code management of clusters, jobs, Unity Catalog, and all Databricks resources. [databricks, terraform]
- [ ] **[databricks/cli – Databricks CLI](https://github.com/databricks/cli)** - Modern Databricks CLI with bundle support for deploying Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs), replacing the legacy CLI with a YAML-first workflow. [databricks]
- [ ] **[databrickslabs/dqx – Databricks data quality](https://github.com/databrickslabs/dqx)** - Databricks Labs data quality framework for profiling and monitoring Delta tables, integrating natively with Delta Live Tables and Unity Catalog. [databricks]
- [ ] **[databrickslabs/lakebridge – SQL migration tool](https://github.com/databrickslabs/lakebridge)** - Databricks Labs tool for migrating SQL workloads from legacy warehouses (Teradata, SQL Server) to Databricks, with automated dialect translation. [databricks]
- [ ] **[databrickslabs/dlt-meta – Delta Live Tables metadata](https://github.com/databrickslabs/dlt-meta)** - Metadata-driven framework for building Delta Live Tables pipelines declaratively from configuration files rather than Python/SQL code. [databricks]
- [ ] **[Eliminating Ingestion Tax with Databricks Lakeflow Connect](https://medium.com/@balajij8/eliminating-ingestion-tax-with-databricks-lakeflow-connect-4d417b4eea63?source=rss------dbt-5)** - How Databricks Lakeflow Connect eliminates the data engineering overhead of building and maintaining ingestion pipelines from common SaaS sources. [databricks]
- [ ] **[A New Era of Databases: Lakebase](https://www.databricks.com/blog/what-is-a-lakebase)** - Databricks introduces "Lakebase" as a new database category combining the transactional capabilities of OLTP databases with the analytical power of the Lakehouse. [databricks]
- [ ] **[Lakehouse Architecture Explained: Replacing Warehouses and Lakes](https://medium.com/@shubhamjadhav04/lakehouse-architecture-explained-the-data-system-thats-replacing-warehouses-and-lakes-6f0ed643acac?source=rss------databricks-5)** - A clear explanation of Lakehouse architecture and why it is becoming the default for organizations that previously ran separate data warehouses and data lakes. [databricks]
- [ ] **[Medallion Architecture: The Backbone of Modern Data Platforms](https://medium.com/@Gayathri_krish/medallion-architecture-the-backbone-of-modern-data-platforms-61e282de0570?source=rss------databricks-5)** - A guide to Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion architecture patterns on Databricks, including when to add or skip layers based on data complexity. [databricks]
- [ ] **[Unity Catalog in Practice: Governance with Databricks](https://medium.com/@kirankbs/unity-catalog-in-practice-governing-streaming-batch-and-ml-on-databricks-5f2e49dfc2bd?source=rss------databricks-5)** - Practical guide to implementing Unity Catalog for unified governance across streaming, batch, and ML workflows on Databricks. [databricks]
- [ ] **[Anomaly detection in Databricks as an extra set of eyes over business tables](https://medium.com/@amitdassit/anomaly-detection-in-databricks-acts-as-an-extra-set-of-eyes-over-our-business-tables-e179b45dee48?source=rss------databricks-5)** - How to implement automated anomaly detection on Databricks Delta tables using statistical models to catch data quality issues before they reach downstream consumers. [databricks]
- [ ] **[googleapis/python-bigquery-dataframes – BigQuery DataFrames](https://github.com/googleapis/python-bigquery-dataframes)** - Google's official pandas-compatible DataFrame library that runs computations directly on BigQuery, bringing familiar Python syntax to warehouse-scale data. [bigquery]
- [ ] **[The Backbone Of BigQuery: How ZetaSQL Standardizes Google's Data Universe](https://medium.com/@kp9810113/the-backbone-of-bigquery-how-zetasql-standardizes-googles-data-universe-fa3150f32503?source=rss------bigquery-5)** - A deep dive into ZetaSQL, the query engine underpinning BigQuery, Cloud Spanner, and other Google data products, explaining how dialect standardization enables cross-product SQL portability. [bigquery]
- [ ] **[BigQuery Graph Series Part 3: Query and Visualize your Graph](https://medium.com/google-cloud/bigquery-graph-series-2e35bb203aac?source=rss------bigquery-5)** - Third installment showing how to query graph data stored in BigQuery using GRAPH SQL syntax and visualize the results with Google's native tooling. [bigquery]
- [ ] **[BigQuery Graph Series Part 2: Build a BigQuery Graph from Unstructured Data](https://medium.com/google-cloud/bigquery-graph-series-6a768ccb351b?source=rss------bigquery-5)** - Tutorial on extracting graph structures from unstructured text using Gemini and storing them in BigQuery's native graph tables. [bigquery]
- [ ] **[BigQuery Data Quality: Scaling Freshness Tests with Stored Procedures](https://medium.com/@chlaeubli/bigquery-data-quality-scaling-freshness-tests-with-stored-procedures-79aa2b8d2d89?source=rss------bigquery-5)** - An approach to scaling data freshness monitoring across hundreds of BigQuery tables using stored procedures and scheduled queries instead of per-table alerting. [bigquery]
- [ ] **[ETL Engineering on Google Cloud, Start to Finish](https://blog.devops.dev/etl-engineering-on-google-cloud-start-to-finish-9da95f606132?source=rss------bigquery-5)** - A complete walkthrough of building production ETL pipelines on Google Cloud with BigQuery as the destination, covering Dataflow, Cloud Storage, and orchestration patterns. [bigquery, gcp]
- [ ] **[googleapis/genai-toolbox – GenAI tools for databases](https://github.com/googleapis/genai-toolbox)** - Google's open-source MCP server and toolbox for integrating LLMs with databases including BigQuery, AlloyDB, and Cloud SQL. [bigquery, mcp]
- [ ] **[mindsdb/mindsdb – ML for existing databases](https://github.com/mindsdb/mindsdb)** - Platform for building ML models and AI agents directly inside existing databases including BigQuery and Snowflake, without moving data out for training. [bigquery, snowflake]
- [ ] **[Data Platforms with Bruin](https://dev.to/derrickryangiggs/data-platforms-with-bruin-2k3k)** - Introduction to Bruin, an open-source data platform tool that integrates ingestion, transformation, and testing for BigQuery and other warehouses in a single CLI. [bigquery]

## Infrastructure & Security

- [ ] **[Don't use passkeys for encrypting user data](https://blog.timcappalli.me/p/passkeys-prf-warning/)** - A technical warning from Tim Cappalli about using the passkey PRF extension for client-side data encryption, detailing edge cases where key derivation can silently fail or produce different keys across authenticators. [security]
- [ ] **[A new California law says all operating systems need age verification](https://www.pcgamer.com/software/operating-systems/a-new-california-law-says-all-operating-systems-including-linux-need-to-have-some-form-of-age-verification-at-account-setup/)** - California passed legislation requiring OS-level age verification at account setup, affecting Linux distributions and raising significant privacy and implementation concerns. 478 HN points. [security, policy]
- [ ] **[OpenAI agrees with Dept. of War to deploy models in classified network](https://www.reuters.com/business/openai-reaches-deal-deploy-ai-models-us-department-war-classified-network-2026-02-28/)** - OpenAI finalized an agreement to deploy frontier models on US Department of Defense classified networks, marking a major shift in frontier AI's role in national security. [ai, security]
- [ ] **[GitHub Actions is left vulnerable to supply chain attacks: Datadog Report](https://www.datadoghq.com/state-of-devsecops/)** - Datadog's State of DevSecOps report reveals systemic GitHub Actions vulnerabilities enabling supply chain compromise through unpinned action versions and insecure workflow configurations. [devops, security]

## Others

- [ ] **[Statement on the comments from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth](https://www.anthropic.com/news/statement-comments-secretary-war)** - Anthropic's formal response to Secretary Hegseth's public comments, addressing AI safety positioning and the company's approach to government relationships. 650 HN points. [ai, policy]
- [ ] **[President Trump bans Anthropic from use in government systems](https://www.npr.org/2026/02/27/nx-s1-5729118/trump-anthropic-pentagon-openai-ai-weapons-ban)** - NPR reports the Trump administration banned Anthropic products from US government use, reportedly over concerns about the company's safety-focused public positioning. 168 HN points. [ai, policy]
- [ ] **[NASA announces overhaul of Artemis program amid safety concerns and delays](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nasa-artemis-moon-program-overhaul/)** - NASA announced a significant restructuring of the Artemis lunar program, citing safety issues and schedule slippage as the program faces political and technical pressure. 230 HN points. [science, space]
- [ ] **[Qt45: A small polymerase ribozyme that can synthesize itself](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.adt2760)** - Scientists published a ribozyme capable of self-replication in Science, a significant step toward understanding the RNA World hypothesis for the origin of life. 63 HN points. [science, biology]
- [ ] **[Croatia declared free of landmines after 31 years](https://glashrvatske.hrt.hr/en/domestic/croatia-declared-free-of-landmines-after-31-years-12593533)** - Croatia completed its 31-year landmine clearance effort following the 1991-1995 war, a landmark humanitarian achievement. 29 HN points. [news]

## Highlights

**OpenAI's $110B raise** at a $730B valuation is unprecedented in private markets. If the capital is deployed into compute, it could widen the gap between frontier labs and competitors significantly over the next 18 months. It also raises serious questions about what "non-profit mission" means at this financial scale.

**The GitHub Copilot CLI malware execution vulnerability** from PromptArmor demonstrates that AI coding assistants are becoming high-value targets for prompt injection attacks. As these tools gain more system-level permissions, the attack surface expands dramatically — security teams need to treat AI coding tools as privileged execution environments.

**Databricks' "Lakebase"** announcement signals a strategic push to capture OLTP workloads alongside the existing Lakehouse analytical use cases. If Databricks can credibly serve transactional queries at the latency required by applications, the traditional distinction between operational databases and analytical platforms collapses — with significant implications for the database market.
